Aspecto: I called the lady who sold me that he receive me because there is a lot of leftover material and he knew how to tell me that he cannot receive it

It is somewhat unfair. And it is true that the service is terrible. The saleswomen should tell the clients that if there is excess, there is no refund to get a quote from the architect who is doing the work and not from the ladies who serve.

Aspecto: I accessed the park from loja
Evidencia:
I accessed the park from Loja, it is essential to go by car or taxi. From the bottom to the refuge it is an 8 kilometer climb, it is better for the taxi to take you there (it cost us $10, then we talked to him to come pick us up)
From the Loja part you have 3 trails to do. One of 700m, another of 300m and the one with the viewpoints which are 5 kilometers. This is the one we did. It is essential to wear boots and be well dressed (if you go in August at least) since it rains up there no matter what, and there are muddy patches that reach your knees.
The access part through Zamora is much better.

Aspecto: Oldest and classic theaters in the city of loja
Evidencia:
One of the best, oldest and classic theaters in the city of Loja, which was remodeled a couple of years ago; its façade completely and its interior partially. This theater presents a variety of plays, dance and music throughout the year and is also home to the Loja Sobre Tablas festival and one of the venues for the International Festival of Living Arts.

A must visit for locals and tourists.

Aspecto: Tourist project is a replica of the shield sent by king philip ii in 1571 to the southern city
Evidencia:
A monument of wonder

Tourist project is a replica of the shield sent by King Philip II in 1571 to the southern city. Its entrance is the meeting point for locals and strangers. It also represents direct access to the Ecuadorian Amazon.

Since its construction in 1998, the City Gate monument in La Loja (southern Andean region) is the meeting point for locals and strangers and the official entrance to this city. Its architecture, art and craft stores make it the most visited area by tourists.

On the other hand, the monument is on the Malacatos River, whose slope joins the Zamora River at an intersection that can be seen from the bridge of this tourist project.

Since its construction in 1998, the City Gate monument in La Loja (southern Andean region) is the meeting point for locals and strangers and the official entrance to this city. Its architecture, art and craft stores make it the most visited area by tourists.

On the other hand, the monument is on the Malacatos River, whose slope joins the Zamora River at an intersection that can be seen from the bridge of this tourist project.

Aspecto: An emblematic and unparalleled place in loja
Evidencia:
An emblematic and unparalleled place in Loja,
Conceived to pay tribute to the independence of the city and its area of ​​influence, an event that occurred on November 18, 1820, it is considered one of the most outstanding attractions of the city.
The tower, 32 meters high and in the Art Deco style, is crowned by a public clock with four dials, one on each side of the structure. At its base you can see four bronze reliefs that recall the history of the Lojano territory: when it was inhabited by the Paltas and Bracamoros, when it formed the Spanish governorate of Maynas and Yaguarzongo.

It is a cozy place, full of happiness.

Aspecto: Very nice parques nacionales although it could be better maintained and signposted
Evidencia: Very nice Parques Nacionales although it could be better maintained and signposted. The entrance via the road to Vilcabamba is not bad, there is a control where you have to register, but entry is free. I recommend going by car to the first shelter, because there are loose dogs on the road. Once at the refuge there are three trails that can be taken, one steep and longer that reaches a viewpoint at 3000m and another two simple and short ones. I recommend doing the hardest one first if you are going to do all three trails. In total it will be 2 hours. Remember to bring warm, waterproof clothing and water, and maybe some energizing food. The weather is cold and the altitude can affect some people. The view and nature make this visit worth it.

The weather is cold and the altitude can affect some people. The view and nature make this visit worth it.

Aspecto: One on each side of the structure
Evidencia: The tower, 32 meters high and in the Art Deco style, is crowned by a public clock with four dials, one on each side of the structure. At its base you can see four bronze reliefs that recall the history of the Lojano territory: when it was inhabited by the Paltas and Bracamoros, when it established the Spanish governorate of Maynas and Yaguarzongo, the Independence of 1820, and finally when the Federal Government was established. of Loja during the political crisis that almost brought Ecuador to the brink of its disappearance.

Aspecto: The gate to enter the city of loja looks like a castle
Evidencia:
The gate to enter the city of Loja looks like a castle, very picturesque.
Inside there is a cafe and a museum. If you climb the stairs to the top of the tower you can see all the horizons of the city.
Some emblematic buildings too.
Before the entrance there is a monument representing a coffee cup and a filter.
You can park your car at the back of the door, but there are very few spaces. We had to go to one of the streets across the avenue.
We were also able to use a bathroom inside the museum.

Aspecto: It formed the first two parishes of the city
Evidencia:
The parish church of El Valle in the municipality of Loja, dates back to the 17th century when Cacique Quinto Alonso Pinza founded the town of San Juan de El Valle in 1629. Together with San Sebastián, it formed the first two parishes of the city. from Loja.
The exact date of the construction of the Church of El Valle is not known, but it is known that between the years 1642-1645 it was a chapel.

Aspecto: Constitutively given in each personal realization
Evidencia: The Church as a social space of faith is much more than the simple gathering of believers. As a "vital" space of faith, it is constitutively given in each personal realization, as a sacrament of the presence and action of Christ and the Spirit, and therefore, "as the foundation of the possibility of personal and community faith." The Church transmits the common faith to each believer and articulates it in each of them on a personal and community basis, as a space for encounter with the God revealed by Jesus Christ. This means that the Church is a sacrament, that is, a sign and instrument of communion with God and men. Let us now look at the theological foundations of these statements.

Aspecto: The famous city gate in loja-ecuador has a cafeteria on the ground floor that you will love (you can search for more information as "la llorona café")
Evidencia:
The famous city gate in Loja-Ecuador has a cafeteria on the ground floor that you will love (you can search for more information as "La Llorona Café"); and a restaurant on the third floor, with tasty typical food (you can search for more information such as "Rincón Borgoña Restaurant").

In addition, it has a museum and the entrance to the towers on the left side. I recommend doing the entire tour so you can appreciate nature and part of the city of Loja, it is worth it.

Something important, the museum has different painting and art exhibitions every season, you can find out about the dates in the same place.

Aspecto: The park: on november 18
Evidencia:
It is made up of several elements:

San Sebastián Church: It was built in 1900 and remodeled in 1979, the church is made up of three naves, an altarpiece in each of them, in the upper part there are two handrails that surround the internal part of the church and are They support with semicircular arches.

The Park: on November 18, 1820, the people of Loja met in this square to proclaim Independence from the Spanish Crown. In the center of the park stands a 32 m high tower, at the top of which is a four-sphere clock and at its base we find four reliefs that indicate the most outstanding events in the history of Loja.

San Sebastián Glorieta: a beautiful traditional architectural style roundabout has been built for cultural events. Old-style buildings surround the park, with wide portals, carved wooden balconies and low eaves, the same ones found within the urban regeneration. developed over recent years.

Aspecto: In addition to generating clean energy for the city
Evidencia:
The Villonaco Wind Power Plant, an emblematic project that, in addition to generating clean energy for the city, has become a highly visited tourist space.

Since 2013, Villonaco began its commissioning and became the first plant of this type in continental Ecuador.

After being closed due to the health emergency, the Central reopened its doors to tourists thanks to the joint work between CELEC – EP GENSUR, the Ministry of Tourism and other entities, who outlined the guidelines to correctly provide all biosecurity measures and Train staff in the knowledge and application of protocols and hospitality.

Visiting this important resource will allow you to learn more about the experience of generating renewable energy. In its Interpretation Center, through photographic exhibitions and videos you can see the entire project, from when the placement of the wind turbines was generated.

In addition, the visit is complemented with tours along its trails and natural viewpoints, which offer a spectacular panoramic view of the city. It also has a tourist cafeteria, where typical Loja snacks such as tamales, humitas and exquisite coffee are sold.

Welcome to this small reflection of my society...

Aspecto: They don't know how much i love my city
Evidencia:
Indicates the site where independence from the Spanish Crown was proclaimed on November 18, 1820.
November 18, 2023. Today we celebrate 203 years of emancipation. They don't know how much I love my city. How I would like it to continue to be the same city that I could once move around peacefully. To date, I have noticed the presence of "strange beings" that are harming him. I can no longer circulate safely or enjoy its anniversaries as in the past. I hope and trust that this nightmare ends.
In any case, I would like to express my congratulations on your 203rd birthday.
Health dear and well-behaved City of the Immaculate Conception of Mercadillo.

Aspecto: The place was incredible
Evidencia:
The place was incredible, except that we went and the weather was horrible so we couldn't go around. It would be nice if there were people 24 hours a day. We arrived after check-in time and there was no one, luckily we slept in a cabin that was open but we were very cold because there was a storm.

From Loja you take a bus to Vilcabamba and get off at the park entrance, it costs approximately 0.75dol. Otherwise you can take a taxi from the Loja terminal or wherever you like, but from there it costs about 10dol and if not to the refuge (above) 18dol. From the entrance without an 8.5km climb to the refuge, they have been walking for a while. Admission is from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m., I recommend arriving earlier because at 5 p.m. there is no one there. The shelter has cabins, a camping area, administration, an open kitchen with a patrol car, bathrooms and showers without hot water. the night costs 3dol. If you can save sleeping there, great, it's best to arrive early in the morning. There are 4 trails, one of 17 minutes, another of 20', another of 3-4 hours (5km) and the longest of 14.5km that takes two days and you have to go with a guide no matter what, it is very difficult and camp in the middle. You have to go warm because in January it is very cold! It climbs to about 3,500 meters high. Incredible! I hope to help you, good luck! Is beautiful!

Aspecto: El pedestal church is one of the city's heritage buildings
Evidencia:
El Pedestal church is one of the city's heritage buildings, it was built in 1927 with the collaboration of Redemptorist missionaries. At first the religious parish was called Immaculate Conception.
The temple is in a neoclassical style, the fine wood fabric can be seen on the roof, as indicated by Father Juan Hidalgo.
Pictures that refer to the passion and death of Jesus are exhibited inside the church.  The religious pieces and paintings date back approximately 100 years, these accessories reflect divinity and holiness.
The parish festivities are carried out with the contribution of the residents of the neighborhood and faithful Catholics. Religious celebrations are part of these festivities.
Currently, 20 catechists give talks to 200 children about first communion. The parish priest calls on citizens not to lose the value of faith and hope.

Aspecto: Beautiful place
Evidencia:
Beautiful place. The climate varies with respect to Loja, not always that it is sunny in Loja and also in the Podocarpus. Better to go in the morning to reach the viewpoint before 10 when it is clear.
From Loja to the entrance of the park it is 15 minutes, from the entrance to the first shelter it is 15 to 20 minutes by car. From there there is a main path to the viewpoint that lasts around 1h30 round trip with medium-low difficulty. There are two other trails of low difficulty that last approximately 20 minutes each.
You can take a bus to Vilcabamba for 2 dollars and it passes through the park entrance. Or else a taxi but it should be clarified that they take them to the 1st refuge. It's $8 one way just to the entrance.

Beautiful place. The climate varies with respect to Loja, not always that it is sunny in Loja and also in the Podocarpus. Better to go in the morning to reach the viewpoint before 10 when it is clear.
From Loja to the entrance of the park it is 15 minutes, from the entrance to the first shelter it is 15 to 20 minutes by car. Beautiful place, you must visit it.  It is reached by road to Vilcabamba, 30 minutes from the center of Loja.
You must register at the checkpoint and then 40 minutes by car you arrive at the Cajanuma shelter.  From there you can take some trails according to your time and physical condition.
Please do not litter in the Park.  Bring the waste you generate back to the city.  Love and know your country .  God bless you

Aspecto: Sent by king felipe ii in 1571 to the city considered the entrance to the amazon
Evidencia: It was built in 1998. It is an architectural monument that constitutes a replica of the Shield of Loja, sent by King Felipe II in 1571 to the city considered the entrance to the Amazon. And entering the city is like moving to a small Castle, if your plans include visiting Vilcabamba, I recommend visiting this place that always has art exhibitions and on the second floor there is a restaurant where you can try traditional dishes from the area and its Exquisite coffee, The view of the mountain city will disappoint as well as its beautiful architecture.

Sincerely,
Svieta

Aspecto: The puerta de la ciudad museos was built in 1999
Evidencia:
This information is from the page of the municipality of Loja, "The Puerta de la Ciudad Museos was built in 1999, but began operating in 2001, it stands on the Bolívar Bridge, inside there are three exhibition rooms. temporary exhibitions, which have hosted local, national and foreign artists who have delighted the community with exhibitions of painting, sculpture and crafts; it is important to note that these exhibitions are held monthly.

The security of the works of art on display is carried out by members of the Urban Security Unit.

"Entry to the facilities of the Puerta de la Ciudad Museos is completely free."

Aspecto: One of the parks in ecuador that we must visit
Evidencia:
It is one of the Parks in Ecuador that we must visit.  Emblematic for the spectacled bear.  The only bear in Latin America and one that is sadly in danger of extinction.
The Podocarpus Parques Nacionales offers you some trails to explore and of varying difficulty.  The simplest is the Spectacled Bear, children and grandparents can do it in 30 minutes round trip.  However, you must be prepared with hiking shoes, a coat, a hat, glasses and water.
The peace and sounds experienced in the place are incredible.  It is a jewel that we must take care of.
To get to the refuge from the main road it takes 30 minutes and it is advisable to go by van.  At the refuge you can camp with prior permission.  At the checkpoint next to the road it is important to register.  The park closes at 4pm.  I suggest you go early and take advantage of the morning to enjoy the view and the tranquility.

Aspecto: Founder of the city of loja
Evidencia: It is a small square surrounded by the Franciscan Church and Convent. In its center is the Monument to Captain Alonso de Mercadillo, founder of the city of Loja, and towards the south-eastern vertex of the square, is the House of Culture of Loja.

Aspecto: In its center is the monument to captain alonso de mercadillo
Evidencia:
It is a small square surrounded by the Franciscan Church and Convent. In its center is the Monument to Captain Alonso de Mercadillo, founder of the city of Loja, and towards the south-eastern vertex of the square, is the House of Culture.

Walking through the colonial center we find the museums, which compile years of history, tradition and exploits. Framed by natural balconies that surround the city and reveal the majesty of its surroundings, placing Villonaco as a sentinel who, from his home, eternal watchman of his children, consents to the development of his lands.

Address
Simón Bolívar and Cristóbal Colón streets.

Attractions
• San Francisco Church: The structure of the first Church of San Francisco was built in 1564, the mayor Pedro Javier Valdivieso rebuilt it after the earthquake of 1749. The building was and is made of adobe like the others. The convent of San Francisco in the city of Loja, because it was a community of missionaries, constituted a strategic point for evangelization both in the territorial jurisdiction of the Paltas and their various towns, as well as in the ethnic groups of the Amazon assigned to the governorates of Yaguarzongo and Mainas.

After the founding of our city, a part of the community of Franciscan brothers settled there for purposes of service to the community and created the Renaissance and neo-Greek style temple in honor of their patron, the same as after the earthquake from 1851, it was rebuilt and maintained to this day together with the monastery of the Franciscan brothers.

• Monument to Alonso de Mercadillo: Captain Alonso de Mercadillo founded the city of Loja in the year 1548 in the Cuxibamba valley. He was born in Loxa in Spain and due to the custom that the conquerors had of giving the name of their native land to the place they conquered, he called our city, “Loja de la Inmaculada Concepción”.

The convent of San Francisco in the city of Loja, because it was a community of missionaries, constituted a strategic point for evangelization both in the territorial jurisdiction of the Paltas and their various towns, as well as in the ethnic groups of the Amazon assigned to the governorates of Yaguarzongo and Mainas.

After the founding of our city, a part of the community of Franciscan brothers settled there for purposes of service to the community and created the Renaissance and neo-Greek style temple in honor of their patron, the same as after the earthquake from 1851, it was rebuilt and maintained to this day together with the monastery of the Franciscan brothers.

• Monument to Alonso de Mercadillo: Captain Alonso de Mercadillo founded the city of Loja in the year 1548 in the Cuxibamba valley. He was born in Loxa in Spain and due to the custom that the conquerors had of giving the name of their native land to the place they conquered, he called our city, “Loja de la Inmaculada Concepción”.

Aspecto: The entrance gate to the city
Evidencia:
It is the Entrance Gate to the city, it represents part of the Shield of Loja, in which you can see a medieval castle, through which you access the city center. Built in the years 1998–1999. It is said to be a replica of the Loja shield.
The entrance itself is made up of the Bolívar Bridge that passes over the Malacatos River, a castle and the sculptures of Don Quixote and his faithful companion, Sancho.
The current entrance to the city of Loja is a creative reproduction of the original coat of arms and enshrines the noble motto of its foundation: "FIDELIS LOXA LIBERIS"
The beginning of a tourist route is the entrance door to the city, a representation based on the Coat of Arms it has, given by King Philip II of Spain in 1571, symbolizing the fortress city, from where the conquest expeditions to the Amazon left. and to the El Dorado Region.
The city gate is one of the most touristic places in the city, it is very beautiful and unique right there we can go to see the paintings on display and even have a coffee in this cold weather.

Aspecto: I was in this place and i was really pleasantly surprised
Evidencia: On my first visit to Loja, I was in this place and I was really pleasantly surprised.  I found a really huge park, full of flora and fauna, crossed by two rivers that later merge into one, areas for skateboarding, bicycles, hiking, rowing, and many children's games in stations that recreate iconic attractions from different countries in the world. world. Sports fields, a swimming pool, ample parking, etc. Without a doubt, a place that is a must-visit for families in the mornings, where you can calmly walk for 2 to 3 hours without getting bored. Free entry. Well maintained, but with some details that could be improved to make it even more attractive (for example, cleaning the artificial lagoon where the rides take place and opening the attractions during the week).

Aspecto: In general it is a rating for the entire city
Evidencia:
In general it is a rating for the entire city.
It is a completely inaccessible city, starting with the bus, a person with a baby carriage cannot access it, much less a person in a wheelchair. Every day we talk about inclusion but it is not practiced. Parks are for the use and enjoyment of older people who sit around talking about topics that might be irrelevant.
There are no places equipped with children's games in which children and youth are going to occupy their minds, so they spend the entire day watching television. And I'm not referring to jipiro and the linear park. There should be three or four play areas adapted by age and with enclosure if they are near main streets, for each neighborhood. Years ago children could play in the streets but today where vehicle drivers drive as if the only ones on the street were them it is unfeasible.
In fact, something super curious happens. And a vehicle can run a red light, almost run over a family and the traffic police officer is in front and doesn't see anything because he is more aware of what they say on their WhatsApp or what's new on their social networks. .
I think they are supposed to be in charge of our security and they are paid for it, their eyes should be on us.
Another thing about Loja is that the sidewalks are extremely thin, in some you cannot walk even two people at the same time, other cars park on the double lanes so there is no space for them to be double lanes (they should be one-way only). ). The vast majority of the city does not have a ️ for people to deposit their garbage when they go down the street, so it ends up on the floor.
In short, there are too many buts, but we will have to start somewhere.

In fact, something super curious happens. And a vehicle can run a red light, almost run over a family and the traffic police officer is in front and doesn't see anything because he is more aware of what they say on their WhatsApp or what's new on their social networks. .
I think they are supposed to be in charge of our security and they are paid for it, their eyes should be on us.
Another thing about Loja is that the sidewalks are extremely thin, in some you cannot walk even two people at the same time, other cars park on the double lanes so there is no space for them to be double lanes (they should be one-way only). ). The vast majority of the city does not have a ️ for people to deposit their garbage when they go down the street, so it ends up on the floor.
In short, there are too many buts, but we will have to start somewhere.

Promoter of the Independence of Loja, a place to walk, sit and enjoy the sunset and be safe.

Aspecto: The art world is frequently nourished by pictographic works and sculptures that highlight the acceptance of one's destiny and redemption by angels
Evidencia:
The temple in honor of the Most Holy Immaculate Virgin of Lourdes, belongs to the Eucharistic and Diocesan Sanctuary of San Sebastián, the church is made up of three naves, an altarpiece in each of them, on the right side is the altar of the Blessed Sacrament and on the On the left side is the Chapel of the Divine Child, but the most striking thing about the church is the image of San Sebastián.
Saint Sebastian is possibly one of the most represented saints of the Iglesias. The art world is frequently nourished by pictographic works and sculptures that highlight the acceptance of one's destiny and redemption by angels.
In the representations of the first millennium he wears the military chlamys as befitted his position, and always beardless. During the Gothic period, he appears with mesh armor in the fashion of the time, but soon he appears with the rich costume of the palatine nobles of that time and generally with a beard. From that moment on, it is much more common to depict him naked at the moment of being shot. The ancient attribute is the wreath of flowers in the hand. The personal attribute, since the Middle Ages, is an arrow and the bow in his hands.
Since the 15th century, artists have preferred to present him naked, young and beardless, with his hands tied to the trunk of a tree behind him and offering his torso to the executioner's arrows. Many artists have represented it; Among them, it is worth highlighting the sculpture by Alonso Berruguete, preserved in the National Museum of Sculpture of Valladolid, and the painting by El Greco, The Martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, one of the most realistic works of this painter, which is in the Cathedral Museum of Palencia.

Saint Sebastian is possibly one of the most represented saints of the Iglesias. The art world is frequently nourished by pictographic works and sculptures that highlight the acceptance of one's destiny and redemption by angels.
In the representations of the first millennium he wears the military chlamys as befitted his position, and always beardless. During the Gothic period, he appears with mesh armor in the fashion of the time, but soon he appears with the rich costume of the palatine nobles of that time and generally with a beard. From that moment on, it is much more common to depict him naked at the moment of being shot. The ancient attribute is the wreath of flowers in the hand. The personal attribute, since the Middle Ages, is an arrow and the bow in his hands.
Since the 15th century, artists have preferred to present him naked, young and beardless, with his hands tied to the trunk of a tree behind him and offering his torso to the executioner's arrows. Many artists have represented it; Among them, it is worth highlighting the sculpture by Alonso Berruguete, preserved in the National Museum of Sculpture of Valladolid, and the painting by El Greco, The Martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, one of the most realistic works of this painter, which is in the Cathedral Museum of Palencia.

Aspecto: Parque nacional podocarpus has several entrances
Evidencia:
Parque Nacional Podocarpus has several entrances. The most visited Cajanuma in Loja, Bombuscaro in Zamora.
Access to Cajanuma is on the Loja - Vilcabamba road, and from there to the refuge it is 8km. At the refuge, if you want to spend the night, you can do so in tents or rent cabins at a super affordable price of $3 p.p. There is a community kitchen, trails, and the park rangers are friendly. Perfect place for hiking. Wear appropriate clothing, height of the shelter above 2700 meters above sea level, maximum height on trails 3400 meters above sea level.

Aspecto: The parque nacional podocarpus is located in two provinces in southern ecuador
Evidencia:
The Parque Nacional Podocarpus is located in two provinces in southern Ecuador, Loja and Zamora Chinchipe. And the interesting thing about this park stands out in its biodiversity and variety of climatic floors that it has. It is megadiverse in birds and plants endemic to the area. It is worth mentioning that entry to the park is free and everything is with the respective signage.
It is very important to mention that the entry of domestic animals into the area is prohibited.

Aspecto: The parque nacional podocarpus is located in an area of ​​great biodiversity and endemism in the southern region of ecuador
Evidencia:
The Parque Nacional Podocarpus is located in an area of ​​great biodiversity and endemism in the southern region of Ecuador. This makes it a clear testimony of the influence of mountains on tropical lands, one of the main reasons for Ecuador's extraordinary biodiversity. Many studies show that most of the country's biodiversity is packed into these intermediate altitudes, where the cold is not as extreme and, instead, humidity, microclimates and geographic barriers abound. The area where Podocarpus is found is considered a site of global importance for the conservation of biodiversity.
For this reason, along with other areas in the area such as the Yacuri Parques Nacionales and the Cerro Plateado Biological Reserve, since 2007 it has been part of the Podocarpus – El Cóndor Biosphere Reserve, a recognition granted by UNESCO. This area is home to a large area of ​​moors, cloud forests and scrub areas, essential for the preservation and continuity of the ecosystems of southern Ecuador and northern Peru.

Aspecto: Jipiro park is located north of the city of loja
Evidencia:
Jipiro Park is located north of the city of Loja, there are several bus lines that cross it due to its large area of ​​land.
This park is considered one of the most representative of the city and diverse of the country, because it has monuments representative of the world and of Ecuadorian cultures.
Inside the park you can rent canoes to go around the lagoon, walk the path that leads to the zoo, horseback riding, play sports such as soccer, ecuavolly, swimming, tennis, cycling. In addition, there is a camping area where picnics could be held.
Regularly on weekends there is a program of typical foods and dances, making it a must-see place in El Paso in the city of Loja.

Aspecto: Jipiro park is a recreational space that is located in the province of loja
Evidencia:
Jipiro Park is a recreational space that is located in the province of Loja. This park is also known as the Cultural University Park because there are replicas of the most important cultural manifestations in the world.

Among the replicas that this park in Ecuador presents, is the replica of Saint Basil's Cathedral, the original of which is located in Moscow. This construction in the park has two slides that make it attractive for children.

Replicas of oriental structures have also been built in the Park, such as the Chinese Pagoda which, unlike the original, is not intended for spiritual and religious purposes, but rather for food purposes, since the complex's food stalls are located in this construction. From this structure, you can also choose to access the boat rental service, one of the most popular activities in the Park.

Aspecto: It has repurposed rooms among which can be mentioned: temporary exhibition hall
Evidencia:
The Music Museos, which opened its doors to the public in 2004 and is located in the first patio of the old Bernardo Valdivieso School; It has repurposed rooms among which can be mentioned: Temporary exhibition hall; cafeteria and a music sales venue. Hosting exhibitions on the biography of composers with a collection that covers nearly 200 years of history, with more than 7,000 scores and 65 instruments on display, especially highlighting composers who have been an icon in the musical field, due to their recognition at local, national and international levels; We can highlight: Salvador Bustamante Celi, Segundo Cueva Celi, Manuel de Jesús Lozano, Carlos Valarezo Figueroa, Marcos Ochoa Muñoz, Víctor Moreno Íñiguez.
One cannot mention the Museos of Music without emphasizing that it is part of the Pío Jaramillo Alvarado Cultural Corporation, an organization made up of several institutions such as: National University of Loja, Bernardo Valdivieso School, House of Ecuadorian Culture, Loja Symphony Orchestra, Conservatory of Music “Salvador Bustamante Celi” and the “Edgar Palacios” and “Nature and Culture” foundations, which since 2000 have worked together to strengthen and disseminate the cultural and artistic expressions of our people.

Aspecto: Due to the restoration work being carried out in the monastery temple
Evidencia:
The Concepistas Museos of Religious Art has been rehabilitated as an occasional chapel since last Monday, due to the restoration work being carried out in the Monastery temple.
The old temple of the Monastery, inaugurated in 1705 and open as a Museos since 2006, has been rehabilitated as an occasional chapel since last Monday, June 4, until the restoration work is completed, approximately at the end of August of this year.

Aspecto: A commemorative structure located in the san sebastián (or independence) square
Evidencia:
The Loja Independence Monument, also known as the San Sebastián Clock Tower, is a commemorative structure located in the San Sebastián (or Independence) Square, in the city of Loja. Conceived to pay tribute to the independence of the city and its area of ​​influence, an event that occurred on November 18, 1820, it is considered one of the most outstanding attractions of the city.

Description
The tower, 32 meters high and in Art Deco style, is crowned by a public clock with four dials, one on each side of the structure. At its base you can see four bronze reliefs that recall the history of the Lojano territory: when it was inhabited by the avocados and bracamoros, when it established the Spanish government of Maynas and Yaguarzongo, the Independence of 1820, and finally when the Federal Government was established. of Loja during the political crisis that almost brought Ecuador to the brink of its disappearance.

Aspecto: From the entrance to the park to the refuge
Evidencia: From the entrance to the Park to the Refuge, it is 8.5 km. The stone path is in good condition and well signposted... And the landscape is magnificent, without equal. From the refuge, there are signs indicating access to the trails. This is reassuring! The trails are in good conditions and of average difficulty (from my point of view). The flora is exuberant. If it's clear, the view from the viewpoints must be superb... We weren't so lucky, too bad for us! That's how it is when you walk in nature! The park team at the entrance was very professional and friendly. It is one of the rare parks in Ecuador where we feel that there is maintenance and all the human and professional work that accompanies the beauty of the protected place.

Aspecto: Here is a proposal for the google photos review of the loja cathedral:
Evidencia:
Here is a proposal for the Google Photos review of the Loja Cathedral:

"The **Cathedral of Loja**, also known as the **Cathedral-Parish of El Sagrario**, is one of the most emblematic architectural treasures of the city. Located in the heart of Loja, in front of the Central Park, this imposing structure is a mandatory stop for those who want to know the history and faith of the region. Just 70 km from the famous Sanctuary of Our Lady of El Cisne, the cathedral combines art and devotion in a space full of tranquility and beauty. .

Aspecto: This space has enough potential to be one of the best parks in southern ecuador
Evidencia:
Anchored: this site is wonderful, it has fantastic dimensions, that's why I don't understand why it's still stuck in the past.
This space has enough potential to be one of the best parks in southern Ecuador, however it is only a park with stone treadmills and loud swings.
I think that the mayor could hold a meeting with preschool teachers and children to explain what they would like from a park. Because currently Jipiro has stopped being a park for children and has only served for the adult population.
It needs surveillance, renovation and I am not referring to putting new bricks or more cement on the roads, it needs to be brought to 2023, it is urgent.

It needs surveillance, renovation and I am not referring to putting new bricks or more cement on the roads, it needs to be brought to 2023, it is urgent.

Aspecto: Being the first theme park in ecuador
Evidencia: Being the first theme park in Ecuador, it handles several scenarios and also has some famous buildings in the world such as the Taj Mahal, the Eiffel Tower, Big Ben or the Inca Pyramid, as well as recreational areas for adults and children, as well as a swimming pool, It even has a pond where you can feed ducks, geese and watch flamingos. As an added value, the Zamora and Jipiro rivers pass through the place. It is 5 minutes from the Terrestrial Terminal and is a must-see site in Loja. 100% recommended!

Aspecto: When you step into the center of the plaza de la independencia lojana
Evidencia: When you step into the center of the Plaza de la Independencia Lojana, the voices and cries of Freedom delivered to heaven on November 18, 1820 will undoubtedly come into your life. The high portals, the carved wooden balconies and the neighboring houses with that colonial style will transport you to the time of floats, horsemen, serenades, knights and damsels. Sitting down to enjoy a Loja coffee at the foot of the Tower of more than thirty meters that emerges gloriously and always accompanied by the pool where you can make a wish for love, has no comparison. I love this place in Loja because it always inspires you to dream higher. And that Freedom is only a cry away from Independence.

Aspecto: Free access
Evidencia: Free access. At the entrance you must register at the office with your name, age and nationality. From the entrance you have to go to the shelter. You can go by car or someone can offer to take you, people are nice. I personally did the climb on foot (walking on dirt and wide roads) and it has some cool panoramic views that you don't appreciate by car/car. It took me approximately 1 hour 30. Once you arrive at the shelter, you can ask about the routes and times. I did the route to the viewpoints in reverse, since they normally recommend doing it the other way around since it takes less (3 hours). However, it was great that way and it also took about 3 hours. It took me 25 minutes to get to the first viewpoint but without stopping and it is physical, so the climb can be delayed up to 45 minutes. Once there, it is up and down through the rest of the viewpoints until there is a steep climb that requires using your hands to climb slightly (there are ropes installed to facilitate the ascent) to the last viewpoint that is on the highest peak. The magnificent views. The normal descent without problem.

Evidencia: Free access. At the entrance you must register at the office with your name, age and nationality. From the entrance you have to go to the shelter. You can go by car or someone can offer to take you, people are nice. I personally did the climb on foot (walking on dirt and wide roads) and it has some cool panoramic views that you don't appreciate by car/car. It took me approximately 1 hour 30. Once you arrive at the shelter, you can ask about the routes and times. I did the route to the viewpoints in reverse, since they normally recommend doing it the other way around since it takes less (3 hours). However, it was great that way and it also took about 3 hours. It took me 25 minutes to get to the first viewpoint but without stopping and it is physical, so the climb can be delayed up to 45 minutes. Once there, it is up and down through the rest of the viewpoints until there is a steep climb that requires using your hands to climb slightly (there are ropes installed to facilitate the ascent) to the last viewpoint that is on the highest peak. The magnificent views. The normal descent without problem.

Aspecto: Although the view of the place is incredible and it is an excellent place to observe all the native flora of the sector
Evidencia:
Although the view of the place is incredible and it is an excellent place to observe all the native flora of the sector.

The abandonment of the authorities is clearly perceived, because the entire road is full of potholes and sinkholes that are dangerous for vehicles. When you walk through the area, you see a large amount of garbage (bottles, potato chip covers, diapers, construction waste, among other things). A shame for the ecosystem and tourism.

How to get there? Take the bus to Bolonia in the "El León" sector (Av. Manuel Agustín Aguirre y Alonso de Mercadillo). Going down from the last stop along the diagonal steps to the communal house. You can go up on foot or take a taxi. The journey on foot will take 2 hours.

Make sure to watch out for passing cars. Many of them are speeding.

Evidencia: Simón Bolívar Park in Loja is a true treasure of the city! ✨ This beautiful space not only offers a peaceful environment to relax and enjoy nature, but it is also a place full of history and culture.
